nodes.name: find_fof_clusters
nodes.description: Computes friends-of-friends (FOF) clusters for a set of points on a periodic grid. This is a core clustering algorithm used in cosmology and astrophysics to identify gravitationally bound structures.

inputs.name: x
inputs.type_desc: Array of particle positions, typically of shape (n_particles, n_dims).

inputs.name: b
inputs.type_desc: The linking length, a scalar value defining the maximum distance for two particles to be considered 'friends'.

inputs.name: L
inputs.type_desc: The size of the periodic box, a scalar or array matching the number of dimensions.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.type_desc: A string or enum indicating the specific algorithm or mode to use for the computation.

inputs.name: max_neighbors
inputs.type_desc: The maximum number of neighbors to consider for each particle, used to pre-allocate memory.

inputs.name: batch_size
inputs.type_desc: The number of particles to process in a single batch to manage memory usage.

outputs.name: clusters
outputs.type_desc: An array of cluster labels, where each element corresponds to a particle in the input array 'x'.
outputs.constraints: The output array will have a length equal to the number of particles.

nodes.type_signature: (x: Array of particle positions, typically of shape (n_particles, n_dims)., b: The linking length, a scalar value defining the maximum distance for two particles to be considered 'friends'., L: The size of the periodic box, a scalar or array matching the number of dimensions., mode: A string or enum indicating the specific algorithm or mode to use for the computation., max_neighbors: The maximum number of neighbors to consider for each particle, used to pre-allocate memory., batch_size: The number of particles to process in a single batch to manage memory usage.) -> An array of cluster labels, where each element corresponds to a particle in the input array 'x'.
nodes.conceptual_summary: transitive_proximity_partitioning. Partitions a set of points in an N-dimensional space with periodic boundaries into disjoint subsets. The partitioning is based on constructing a proximity graph where an edge exists between any two points if their distance is below a specified threshold. Each output subset represents a connected component of this graph, effectively forming a transitive closure over the 'is-a-neighbor' relationship.. Applications: Molecular Dynamics: Identifying groups of interacting atoms or molecules in a simulation with a periodic solvent box to study aggregation or complex formation., Epidemiology: Modeling contagion clusters in a population distributed over a geographical area with wrap-around boundaries, where the linking length represents the transmission radius., Materials Science: Analyzing the formation of crystal grains or the propagation of defects within a simulated material lattice that uses periodic boundary conditions., Sensor Networks: Grouping signals from a distributed network of sensors deployed in a toroidal or wrap-around physical space to identify event regions.
